what: Cloudflare Worker — the Slack / Airtable front door
title: How Jinx is built
text: worker https://jinx.rladies.workers.dev lives worker/src/ piece Jinx run GitHub infrastructure. exists Slack Airtable need low-latency HTTPS endpoint Actions provide. Five things happen : OAuth token storage — slack-oauth.js runs install flow stashes per-workspace bot tokens Cloudflare KV (SLACK_TOKENS). allowlist check (organisers + community workspaces ) rejects install outside RLadies+. Signature verification — every Slack-side POST runs slack_signature_verify() slack-api.js business logic. Slash commands mentions — slash-command.js slack-events.js translate Slack payloads repository_dispatch events fire matching bot-* workflow, post workflow’s reply back via response_url. Slack sees normal slash-command response; GitHub sees normal workflow run. RAG answers — rag.js handles @Jinx-mention questions querying Cloudflare Vectorize index RLadies+ Guide website, using Workers AI answer. path stays entirely inside worker — GitHub round-trip. Airtable invite approval — airtable-invite.js receives webhook public invite form posts Block Kit cards organisers’ channel; slack-interact handles Approve / Deny / Mark-sent button clicks. worker deployed infra-deploy-worker.yml configured Wrangler secrets KV bindings, documented .github/AGENTS.md.

title: RAG indexer
text: indexer runs week (demand) GitHub Actions workflow. run : Reads list sources inst/config/rag-sources.yml. source, calls matching gather_<type>() function produce list text chunks. Embeds chunks batches Cloudflare Workers AI (@cf/baai/bge-base-en-v1.5). Upserts vectors rladies-content Cloudflare Vectorize index. Vectors keyed sha256("{repo}|{path}|{chunk_idx}")[1:32]. Re-running indexer idempotent — content produces IDs, existing vectors updated place.

title: RAG indexer
text: chunking, embedding, upsert logic live R/rag-chunk.R, R/rag-embed.R, R/rag-index.R. dispatcher gather_rag_source() finds gather_<type>(src) (dashes type become underscores function name).
code: inst/config/rag-sources.yml         │         ▼    load_rag_sources()         │         ▼   rag_index_build()         │         ├─► gather_rag_source(src)   ── dispatched per `type` ──►  gather_<type>(src)         │                                                                │         │                                                                ▼         │                                                         chunk_markdown()         │                                                                │         ▼                                                                ▼   cloudflare_embed()  ◄── batch of 50 ───────────  list of chunk records         │         ▼   cloudflare_vectorize_upsert()         │         ▼    rladies-content (Cloudflare Vectorize)

title: RAG indexer
text: source ends R/rag-source-<type>.R.

title: RAG indexer
text: Adding source two files one YAML entry. Step 1. Decide type — kebab-case identifier discourse-forum. maps directly function name gather_discourse_forum(). Step 2. Create R/rag-source-discourse-forum.R export (@keywords internal fine) function takes source spec returns list chunk records: chunk record needs fields: text — content embed (≥ 200 chars trimming). heading — section heading within page (may empty). title — page title. repo / path — used part vector ID metadata. pair must stable across runs re-indexing updates existing vector instead orphaning . url — Slack bot link answer. date / lastmod — unix seconds. Used apply staleness factor query time. 0 unknown. chunk_idx — zero-based index within document (sources emit one chunk per item use 0L; sources split long documents use chunk_markdown() assign sequential indices). source produces markdown documents, use chunk_markdown() chunk sizes stay consistent across index: Step 3. Add YAML entry inst/config/rag-sources.yml: Anything put YAML passed gather function src argument. source_type shows retrieved chunk’s metadata — indexer sets every chunk function returns, don’t need set . Step 4. Add tests. Pure formatting/parsing helpers go tests/testthat/test-rag-sources.R; HTTP-touching helpers mocked local_mocked_responses() (put library(httr2) top test file don’t need httr2:: prefix — see test-rag-embed.R pattern). ’s whole contributor surface. don’t touch orchestrator, dispatcher, embed/upsert logic.
code: gather_discourse_forum <- function(src) {   # ... fetch and process ...   list(     list(       text = "the chunk text — at least 200 chars after trimming",       heading = "thread title or section heading",       title = "page title",       repo = src$repo,       path = "thread/1234",       url = "https://forum.example.com/t/1234",       date = 1700000000L,       lastmod = 1700000000L,       chunk_idx = 0L     )   ) } chunks <- chunk_markdown(   markdown_text,   meta = list(     repo = src$repo,     path = "/some/path",     url = "https://example.com/some/path",     fallback_title = "Page title"   ) ) for (i in seq_along(chunks)) {   chunks[[i]]$chunk_idx <- i - 1L } - type: discourse-forum   source_type: forum   repo: example/forum   base_url: https://forum.example.com

title: Operating Jinx
text: workflow run failed. Open Actions tab, find red run, read cli log failing step. module functions print context. Slack /jinx ... got reply. Tail Worker (wrangler tail worker/). Failures almost always signature verification, missing env var, Slack scope wasn’t granted. scheduled job didn’t run. GitHub disables scheduled workflows inactive repos. Trigger one manually Actions UI schedule resumes. new command wired Slack says “unknown command”. bot image hasn’t rebuilt yet — wait infra-build-bot-image finish, try . audit log Actions run history. Logs runs older 14 days gone; need preserve evidence, screenshot retention window closes.

title: RLadies+ GitHub Organisation Management
text: Jinx operations bot RLadies+. runs GitHub Actions jinx[bot], Cloudflare Worker bridging Slack machinery. Organisers reach Jinx two ways — typing /jinx ... Slack, posting /jinx ... comment issue PR RLadies+ repo. Either way, R package answers. Day day, Jinx handles organiser onboarding, directory PR review, chapter monitoring, announcements, reports, event sync, translation checks, Slack invites RLadies+ community.

title: RLadies+ GitHub Organisation Management
text: Jinx ships handful reusable GitHub Actions workflows RLadies+ repo can adopt lines YAML. ’re .github/workflows/ start reusable-:

title: RLadies+ GitHub Organisation Management
text: Every reusable runs inside ghcr.io/rladies/jinx-bot container. runner pull image, calling workflow must grant job packages: read. simplest pattern workflow-level block: Without , ’ll see Error response daemon: denied docker pull step job fails welcome/thank/checklist runs. JINX_APP_ID JINX_PRIVATE_KEY configured org-level secrets rladies, repo org inherits automatically — per-repo configuration needed secrets . jinx GitHub App need installed calling repo, true org-wide today. minimal caller looks like:
code: permissions:   contents: read   packages: read name: Hello on PR or Issue  on:   pull_request:     types: [opened, closed]   issues:     types: [opened]  permissions:   contents: read   packages: read  jobs:   welcome:     if: github.event.action == 'opened'     uses: rladies/jinx/.github/workflows/reusable-welcome-contributor.yml@main     secrets:       JINX_APP_ID: ${{ secrets.JINX_APP_ID }}       JINX_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.JINX_PRIVATE_KEY }}    thank:     if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.action == 'closed' && github.event.pull_request.merged == true     uses: rladies/jinx/.github/workflows/reusable-thank-contributor.yml@main     secrets:       JINX_APP_ID: ${{ secrets.JINX_APP_ID }}       JINX_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.JINX_PRIVATE_KEY }}

title: RLadies+ GitHub Organisation Management
text: Jinx can hand content reviews GitHub Copilot, guided rladies/grimoire review gates (brand, blog, social, translation). ’s two-step setup per repo: Sync gates — run /jinx copilot-sync <owner/repo> (jinx::copilot_sync_repo()). opens PR adding .github/copilot-instructions.md path-scoped .github/instructions/*.instructions.md Copilot’s review speaks RLadies+ brand voice. Re-run refresh grimoire changes. Wire reusable Copilot asked review every content PR. Copilot reusable needs pull-requests: write (request reviewer) top usual contents: read / packages: read: demand, organisers can also run /jinx review brand|blog|social|translation <pr> (e.g. /jinx review blog rladies/rladies.github.io#42) Slack GitHub comment.
code: name: Copilot review  on:   pull_request:     types: [opened, ready_for_review, synchronize]     paths:       - "content/**"  permissions:   contents: read   packages: read   pull-requests: write  jobs:   copilot-review:     uses: rladies/jinx/.github/workflows/reusable-copilot-review.yml@main     secrets:       JINX_APP_ID: ${{ secrets.JINX_APP_ID }}       JINX_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.JINX_PRIVATE_KEY }}

title: jinx (development version)
text: Jinx now keeps anonymous question-improvement log corpus can evolve. Every @Jinx mention DM records question text coarse outcome (answered, no_match, coding_declined, low_confidence) Cloudflare D1 table — Slack user id, channel, thread timestamp, logged question traced asked. Answers linked 👍/👎 reaction updates question’s score, turning “answers weak” maintainer -list. /jinx questions [days] surfaces top gaps -downvoted answers; rows purged 180 days daily cron. Reading log (via /jinx questions /jinx feedback) restricted global team, reusing Airtable member directory cmd_authorize(). Requires provisioning jinx-question-log D1 database (see wrangler.jsonc). weekly digest turns gaps action list. Every Monday scheduled job reads question log, clusters questions Jinx couldn’t answer well (no_match / low_confidence, excluding declined coding questions), drafts proposed Guide answer top gaps, posts lot — plus 👎’d answers — global-team Slack channel (SLACK_DIGEST_CHANNEL, default #team-jinx) review. corpus stays human-authored: drafts AI-suggested unverified, ever Slack message person confirm editing Guide — nothing written RAG index automatically. Global-team authorization matches Slack member id, handle. cmd_authorize() (worker’s command gate) now compare requesting Slack user_id organiser_slack_id column member directory, rather informal @mention organiser_slack. Handle matching unreliable (nicknames, real names spaces) impossible Enterprise Grid, users.info resolve profiles. bot-commands.yml now passes user_id actor Slack-sourced commands. Populate organiser_slack_id global-team member.

title: jinx (development version)
text: automated review lists clickable profile links instead probing . validate_directory_pr() used HTTP-check whether social handle resolved, platforms block bot requests, nearly every entry flagged (“may resolve”) — noise trained reviewers ignore report. now renders entry’s socials ready-made links reviewer clicks confirm. Also fixes Mastodon URL builder, returned NA normal @user@instance form. directory_sync_airtable() now produces real directory entries. reads live submissions base (appzYxePUruG9Nwyg, table submissions) linked languages, countries, interests tables, writes full entry schema (data/json/<slug>.json), profile photos (data/img/), contact emails (contact/<slug>.json). Returning submitters matched existing entry slug (directory_id, falling back identifier) merged partial update — clear_fields wiped first, submitted fields overlay rest. genuinely changed files committed (order- formatting-insensitive comparison), delete requests reported PR body rather executed. Replaces earlier stub targeted placeholder base wrote name-plus-socials shape. directory sync runs private rladies/directory repo, jinx. Directory submissions carry confidential data (contact emails), jinx public, sync logs must stay private repo, installs jinx calls directory_sync_airtable(). global-team sync also gone, public ops-airtable-sync.yml workflow removed. validate_directory_pr() now real automated review, posting one consolidated comment directory PRs covering filenames, likely-duplicate slugs, contact-method vs. social-entry consistency, stray contact info free text, whether social handles resolve — run private repo’s review workflow. Handle normalisation now also covers github bluesky. bundled directory-entry.json schema now matches full entry shape (location, social media, interests, languages, activities, work, photo).

GitHub commands matched commenter’s login; Slack slash commands matched signed user_name. check fails closed — unknown actors directory lookup failures denied — closing path member allowlisted Slack workspace trigger privileged GitHub actions. New commands privileged default explicitly declared safe. Privileged Slack commands restricted organisers workspace. Slack user_name mutable workspace-scoped, community workspace openly joinable, colliding handle must authorize privileged actions. cmd_authorize() honors privileged Slack commands originating organisers workspace; read-commands unaffected. Command privilege now keyword-labelled handler. Commands declared single registry, tagged jinx_safe jinx_gated next handler; dispatch safe/gated classification derive , test asserts every command carries keyword every parseable action registered. Replaces separate hand-maintained safe-command list, new command labelled defined.

title: jinx (development version)
text: /jinx chapter-health now reports real data. command placeholder echoed “Checking chapter health…”; now runs health check summarises inactive chapters. /jinx blog-add <url> now opens PR. New blog_add_pr() fetches page metadata opens PR adding entry awesome-rladies-creations (skipping domains already listed); command previously echoed URL. /jinx blog-check-links now checks real links. text: First-time contributor check actually works. is_first_time_contributor() passing creator= GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls, API silently ignores — every author looked like first-timer repo one PR total, like returning contributor . Now uses GET /search/issues :pr/:issue + author: count authored items correctly. Workflow-input injection lane closed. ops-chapter-onboarding, ops-global-team-invite, ops-global-team-finalize, ops-global-team-offboard, ops-update-contributors, ops-airtable-sync now move every ${{ inputs.* }} env: block read Sys.getenv() inside Rscript. invite workflow also builds JSON artifact jq instead echo ' name longer crashes run. Two records named “Maria” used collide maria.json. airtable_to_directory_entry() now appends 6-char hash Airtable record id slug. Contributors update stops daily commit churn. equality check used compare rendered file verbatim, including _Last updated: {Sys.Date()}_, always diffed committed daily even contributors changed. check now strips line comparing. is_team_member fails open transient errors. real 404 still returns FALSE, network blip 5xx returns TRUE (warning) don’t spam global team first-timer welcomes GitHub hiccup. review_assign_onboarding surfaces errors. title: jinx 0.1.0
text: Initial release. jinx automates organisational workflows RLadies+ GitHub org: chapter onboarding health, directory validation, blog announcement publishing, PR review, Airtable sync, Slack, analytics, events, CFP coordination, i18n. Exported functions follow <module>_<verb>[_<object>] schema group cleanly autocomplete. Short prefixes used established acronyms (cmd_, gt_, li_, cfp_, gha_, i18n_). Triggered via /jinx issue comments (cmd_parse() / cmd_execute()) 24 GitHub Actions workflows. Ships pkgdown site, Getting Started Workflow Reference vignettes, starter translations Spanish, Portuguese, French.
